pages.text: Conference Meeting - January 6, 2026 ‘The conference Meeting began at 6:41 p.m., the Municipal Clerk read the following statement: | This is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with Section 5 of the | Open Public Meetings Act, Chapter 231, Public Law 1975, The notice for this meeting was emailed to the | Star Ledger and the West Orange Chronicle on January 7, 2025 and published in the West Orange Chronicle on January 16, 2025, Roll Call - Councilwoman Casalino, Councilman Krakoviak Councilwoman Rudin, Councilwoman Scarpa and Councilwoman Williams Couneil President Nomination(s) A motion was made by Councilwoman Casalino and seconded by Councilwoman Rudin, to nominate Councilwoman Susan Scarpa as the 2026 Council President. Councilwoman Casalino, Councilman Krakoviak, Councilwoman Rudin and Councilwoman Scarpa voting “Aye.” Councilwoman Williams abstained. Oath of Office: Council President Susan Scarpa - Administered by Mayor McCartney Council Liaison Appointments On motion made by Councilwoman Casalino and seconded by Councilwoman Williams, the following appointments to the Township Boards, Commissions and Committees were approved. Roll Call — Councilwoman Casalino, Councilman Krakoviak, Councilwoman Rudin, Councilwoman Williams and Council President Scarpa | Pledge of Allegiance | | Public Comment Joe Fagan-Public Information Officer for West Orange Doug Adams ~ Westover Terr. — recognition of Championship Sports and Recreational Teams; Mayor’s Program for the Uniquely Gifted Lisa Adams — Westover Terr. - Mayor’s Program for the Uniquely Gifted | Jacquelyn Hendy ~ Beverly Rd, — Rodent problem in Town; recognition of Muslim American Heritage Month; hateful comments on social media | Justin Goldsman — Moran Rd. ~ Stormwater data Ordinance; Cannabis facility operating hours; hateful comments on social media | Sally Malanga — Ridge Rd. — Expressed opposition to Essex County Deer hunt | Phil Litwinoff — Linden Ave, — Contract for DPW Supervisor | Richard Groves ~ Yale Terr. — Muslim American Heritage Month-hateful comments on social media | John Blanton — Forest Hill Rd. - Hate; Thank you to First Responders; recognition of Championship i Sports and Recreational Teams; opposed to Budget cuts to Mayor's Program for the Uniquely Gifted ‘There was no one else who wished to be heard, and the public comment portion of the agenda was closed. Ordinances of First Reading Ord. 2947-26 On motion made by Councilwoman Williams and seconded by Council President Scarpa, An Ordinance Amending and Supplementing Chapter 7, Traffic, Subsection 7-32.2, Restricted Parking Zones of the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, was approved on first reading, ordered advertised according to law, to come up for second and final reading on January 20, 2026. All members present voting “Aye.” Ord. 2948-25 On motion made by Councilwoman Casalino and seconded by Council President Scarpa, An Ordinance Amending and Supplementing Chapter 7, Traffic, Subsection 7-10.1 Stopping or Standing Prohibited During Certain Hours on Certain Streets, and 7-14.3 Parking Time Limited on Certain Streets During Certain Hours of Certain Days of the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, was approved on first reading, ordered advertised according to law, to come up for second and final reading on January 20, 2026. Councilwoman Casalino, Councilman Krakoviak and Council President Scarpa voting “Aye.” Councilwoman Williams voting “Nay.” Pending Matters/New Matters/Council Discussion A motion was made by Councilwoman Casalino and seconded by Councilwoman Williams, to hear public comment on agenda items only at the beginning of the meeting and non-agenda items near the end of the meeting after all matters on the agenda were discussed and voted on. Councilwoman Casalino and Council President Scarpa voting “Aye.” Councilman Krakoviak and Councilwoman Williams voting “Nay.” The motion FATLED. Special Public Meeting— January 21, 2026 ‘The Special Public meeting began at 4:09 p.m., the Municipal Clerk read the following statement: This is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with Section 5 of the Open Public Meetings Act, Chapter 231, Public Law 1975. ‘The notice for this meeting was emailed to the West Orange Chronicle and the Star Ledger on January 9, 2026 Roll Call — Councilman Krakoviak, Councilwoman Williams and Council President Scarpa Councilwoman Rudin arrived after roll call. Councilwoman Casalino was in route, Pledge of Allegiance Public Comment There was no one else who wished to be heard, and the public comment portion of the agenda was closed. Resolution(s) Removed from Consent Agenda 31-26 Resolution Authorizing an Executive Session for the purpose of discussing matters falling within the attorney-client privilege, to the extent that confidentiality is required in order for the attorney to exercise his ethical duties to this body; and for the discussion of matters which, by express provision of federal law, state statute, or rule of court, are required to be rendered confidential, specifically including: Affordable Housing; Redevelopment of 25 Lakeside Avenue and 55 Lakeside Avenue; and any additional matters of attorney-client privileged information that may arise during discussion. On motion made by Councilwoman Williams and seconded by Council President Scarpa, the above referenced resolution was approved. All members present voting “Aye.” On motion made by Councilwoman Williams and seconded by Councilman Krakoviak, the Council recessed their public meeting and entered into an executive session. All members present voting “Aye.” At 6:59 p.m., the Council resumed their public meeting. On motion made by Councilwoman Williams and seconded by Councilman Krakoviak, the Public meeting was adjourned. All members present voting “Aye.” The Minutes of this meeting are on file in the Office of the Municipal Clerk. if f ) A / Fhe ha Kar ‘arnevgle, R.M.C. /Susan Scarpa 4 s nicipal Clerk Council President Adopted: March 3, 2026 |

The applicants are here because the projects they propose do not meet the requirements of the West Orange Zoning Code, or they have been denied permits by the building inspector. We are their first avenue of appeal. Kelly Carey Esq. - Zoning Board Attorney: Board members have a two-fold responsibility. First, to see that the application… that the applicant is treated as fairly as possible, since no zoning code can foresee all possible inequities. And second, to protect the welfare of the community as a whole, which is the basic purpose of municipal zoning. Kelly Carey Esq. - Zoning Board Attorney: Our procedures are simple. The applicant presents his case. Objectors then present their case. Citizens in the audience are then given the opportunity to speak both for or against the application. We expect the conduct of these proceedings to be dignified and orderly, so that all parties may obtain a fair and impartial hearing. Kelly Carey Esq. - Zoning Board Attorney: As our proceedings are public, so is our decision making, which usually takes place the same evening.
